(e) OpenAlex query is simplified given its internal relevance ranking, retaining the three-block Boolean logic without redundant variants. full_result.refiner_notes: All five warnings from the validation report have been fully resolved. (1) PubMed wildcard fix: 'epidemiolog*' and 'older adult*' are now unquoted in PubMed so wildcard truncation operates correctly; elder* is also added unquoted for broader coverage. (2) PubMed precision: the overly broad standalone 'morbidity'[Title/Abstract] term has been removed from all three queries; specificity is maintained through retained outcome terms such as 'health outcomes', disease-specific terms, and MeSH headings. (3) OpenAlex structure: all four concept blocks remain explicitly parenthesised with AND between blocks; plain phrase strings are used throughout since OpenAlex does not support field tags or wildcard truncation, and two additional plain synonyms ('epidemiology', 'vulnerable populations') replace the non-expanding wildcard forms. (4) Fourth AND block retained but tightened: the population/context block remains as a precision filter with improved specificity terms, supporting subgroup and equity-focused study retrieval without excessive broadening. (5) 'temperature forecast' removed from all queries as it risked retrieving purely meteorological literature unrelated to public health interventions. Additional cross-database harmonisation: 'heat stroke' and 'health outcomes' added to Scopus and OpenAlex outcome blocks for consistency with PubMed MeSH coverage; 'vulnerable population*' added to Scopus; all parentheses verified balanced across all three queries.
